description:
You are a database administrator who is cleaning up a set of dump files before handing them to another team.
All work must be done strictly inside /home/user.

A. Starting condition  
1. The directory /home/user/db_dumps already exists and contains three plain-text SQL files:  
   • customers.sql  
   • orders.sql  
   • inventory.sql  

B. Your goals are to:  
1. Create a compressed archive named db_dumps_backup.tar.gz that contains all three *.sql files.  
   • Put the archive in a **new directory** /home/user/backups  
   • The archive name must be exactly “db_dumps_backup.tar.gz” (all lowercase).  

2. Generate a SHA-256 checksum file for the archive.  
   • Name: /home/user/backups/db_dumps_backup.sha256  
   • The file must contain exactly one line, formatted as:  
     `<64-char-hash>␠␠db_dumps_backup.tar.gz`  
     (i.e. hash, two spaces, then the file name).  
   • Do NOT include any extra spaces or newline characters before/after the single line.  

3. Extract **only** orders.sql from the new archive into a **second new directory** called /home/user/db_work.  
   • Do not extract the other two .sql files into /home/user/db_work.  
   • Do not move or delete the original /home/user/db_dumps directory or its contents.  

4. After the extraction, create a log file /home/user/db_work/extraction.log containing exactly these three lines:  
   Line 1: `orders.sql extracted successfully on 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z`  
   Line 2: `Source archive: /home/user/backups/db_dumps_backup.tar.gz`  
   Line 3: `Destination: /home/user/db_work/orders.sql`  
   (No leading or trailing spaces on any line, and be sure to include the final newline expected by Linux text files.)  

C. Hints (you do NOT have to follow these literally):  
• tar, gzip, and sha256sum are all available.  
• Remember to create the /home/user/backups and /home/user/db_work directories first.  
• You do not need to delete any files when finished.  

D. Automated tests will confirm:  
1. The exact path and name of both new directories.  
2. That /home/user/backups/db_dumps_backup.tar.gz is a valid gzip compressed tar containing customers.sql, orders.sql, and inventory.sql.  
3. That the checksum file exists and follows the precise format described above.  
4. That only orders.sql is present in /home/user/db_work (no other .sql files).  
5. That /home/user/db_work/extraction.log exists and matches the three-line format shown in step B-4 verbatim.

If everything passes, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial files in /home/user/db_dumps:

/home/user/db_dumps/customers.sql
---------------------------------
-- Dump of table customers
INSERT INTO customers (id, name, email) VALUES
(1, 'Alice Smith', 'alice@example.com'),
(2, 'Bob Jones', 'bob@example.com');

/home/user/db_dumps/orders.sql
------------------------------
-- Dump of table orders
INSERT INTO orders (id, customer_id, order_total) VALUES
(1, 1, 99.99),
(2, 2, 149.49);

/home/user/db_dumps/inventory.sql
---------------------------------
-- Dump of table inventory
INSERT INTO inventory (id, product_name, quantity) VALUES
(1, 'Widget', 500),
(2, 'Gadget', 300);

Files the agent must create:

1. Directory: /home/user/backups           (mode 755)
/home/user/backups/db_dumps_backup.tar.gz  (binary gzip-compressed tar)
/home/user/backups/db_dumps_backup.sha256  (single-line text file)

2. Directory: /home/user/db_work           (mode 755)
/home/user/db_work/orders.sql              (exact byte-for-byte copy of the orders.sql listed above)
/home/user/db_work/extraction.log          (three lines, final newline)

Explicit content required for extraction.log:

orders.sql extracted successfully on 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z
Source archive: /home/user/backups/db_dumps_backup.tar.gz
Destination: /home/user/db_work/orders.sql

Checksum verification rule used by the grader:
• The grader will compute `sha256sum /home/user/backups/db_dumps_backup.tar.gz`
  and compare the resulting 64-character hexadecimal digest with the one written
  inside /home/user/backups/db_dumps_backup.sha256.
name: task_000000_d9f2d842
